source_jobs.title: Senior Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Cyvl http://cyvl.com is a Boston-based tech startup revolutionizing how civil engineering firms and governments map and manage transportation infrastructure. Our enterprise-grade hardware and software solutions leverage 3D mapping sensors to capture LiDAR, imagery, and GPS data, retrofitted to our customers' vehicles. This data is processed through our AI-powered cloud pipelines to generate actionable geospatial insights that save cities and engineering firms time, money, and resources.

At Cyvl, our mission is to empower governments to build and maintain public infrastructure they are proud of by accelerating decision-making through our sensors and Infrastructure Intelligence Platform. You will be part of a team of A+ players solving hard, meaningful problems and working directly with leaders at top cities and engineering firms.

We are a fast-moving, high-growth team that believes in solving real-world problems with authenticity, simplicity, and boldness. Every member of our team is empowered to take ownership, deliver results, and leave a lasting impact on the communities we serve.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a hungry, smart, and relentless full-cycle seller who can build and close high-value enterprise and public sector deals. You will own every stage of the sales motion from prospecting to close across city, county, and engineering-firm accounts, engaging directly with executives and multi-stakeholder buying teams. This role offers a front-row seat to how a startup scales from approximately 5M ARR into a 50M+ category leader.

You will partner closely with Product, Marketing, and Customer Success to turn wins into long-term strategic relationships and help elevate the performance of the overall sales function.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build and manage a seven-figure pipeline across city, county, and enterprise accounts.

 - Run disciplined discovery using MEDDICC to qualify, quantify, and close.

 - Navigate multi-stakeholder procurement and RFPs with precision.

 - Consistently close $2M+ in new ARR with ACVs of $150K to 1M+.

 - Forecast accurately and keep CRM spotless.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally and coach junior reps to raise the bar.




QUALIFICATIONS


REQUIRED

 - 3 to 5 years of full-cycle B2B sales experience, ideally in enterprise SaaS or public sector.

 - Proven track record of closing complex six-figure or larger deals.

 - Strong communication skills with both technical and executive-level audiences.

 - Competitive, curious, and highly coachable. Thrives in office environments, on whiteboards, and on the road.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience selling to government agencies or civil engineering firms.

 - Experience in early stage, high-growth startup environments.

 - Familiarity with procurement cycles, RFP processes, and MEDDICC.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage

 - 401(k) with immediate vesting

 - Generous PTO, paid parental leave, and flexible work options

 - Annual professional development stipend

 - Team events, referral bonuses, and a collaborative, growth-focused culture




WHY CYVL

At Cyvl http://cyvl.com, you’ll join a driven and supportive team that’s all out in building technology with real impact. We care deeply about our work, our customers, and each other. Collaboration and trust are at the core of how we operate.

Our values guide how we operate and shape a team that is genuinely RAARE:

 - Real - authentic, transparent, focused on solving real problems with simple, effective solutions.

 - All Out - bold, driven, and committed to delivering results that matter.

 - Accelerating - always learning, growing, and moving with intention.

 - Reliable - we follow through and deliver at a consistently high bar.

 - Empowering - we lift each other up and help governments build infrastructure they can be proud of.

Together, we stay curious, learn quickly, and keep raising the bar. If you want to grow, make an impact that truly matt

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

CYVL https://www.cyvl.com/ is a Physical AI company on a mission to bring Infrastructure Intelligence to every city, town, and community on the planet. We help governments plan, build, and maintain the physical infrastructure that people depend on every day — roads, sidewalks, bridges, utilities, and more — better than ever before.

We pioneered an end-to-end approach that combines purpose-built hardware, computer vision, cloud data pipelines, and AI to transform raw LiDAR and imagery into a living, structured understanding of the built world. Our platform turns what used to take months of manual inspection into actionable intelligence that's easy for any agency to use, saving cities time, money, and resources across hundreds of communities nationwide.

At CYVL, we're building toward a future where every piece of public infrastructure on Earth is captured, understood, and continuously monitored — so the agencies responsible for it and the residents who rely on it can make better decisions, faster. That's the power of Physical AI applied to the real world.

We're a fast-moving, high-growth team based in Boston, MA that believes in solving real-world problems with authenticity, simplicity, and boldness. Every member of our team is empowered to take ownership, deliver results, and leave a lasting impact on the communities we serve.

 
 


ABOUT YOU

You're a seasoned Customer Success Manager who knows how to build customer relationships into lasting partnerships and passionate advocates. At Cyvl, you'll own the full customer lifecycle from onboarding through renewal and play a direct role in shaping how our customers achieve measurable outcomes with our platform. You thrive in a fast-moving startup environment where you're not just following the playbook, you're helping write it. If you're energized by building something from the ground up and love finding smarter ways to work, this role is for you.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead customers through the full lifecycle including onboarding, adoption, expansion, and renewal while serving as their trusted advisor and primary point of contact

 - Engage key stakeholders to define success metrics, track progress, and demonstrate the tangible impact Cyvl is delivering against their goals

 - Deliver hands-on training, strategic business reviews, and some in-person engagements that deepen relationships and drive advocacy

 - Identify and execute expansion opportunities, manage the full renewal cycle, and drive upsells across your book of business

 - Partner cross-functionally with Product and Marketing on discovery sessions, case studies, and customer events

 


QUALIFICATIONS


REQUIRED:

 - 4+ years of Customer Success experience, with a proven track record managing a large book of business and some high-touch customer relationships

 - Demonstrated ability to lead strategic customer interactions including onboarding, QBRs, and renewal cycles

 - Strong analytical mindset; you use data to spot trends, measure impact, and uncover expansion opportunities

 - Builder mentality; excited to help establish CS processes and playbooks in an early-stage startup environment

 - AI-first approach; you actively leverage AI tools to work more efficiently and maximize high-impact customer moments

 - Exceptional communicator, equally effective in writing, on a call, or in the room

 


NICE TO HAVE:

 - Experience working with local governments, municipalities, or public sector organizations of varying sizes

 - Familiarity with infrastructure, field operations, or GIS-adjacent technology

 


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage

 - 401(k) with immediate vesting

 - Generous PTO, paid parental leave, and flexible work options

 - Annual professional development stipend

 - Team events, referral bonuses, and a collaborative, growth-focused culture




OUR VALUES

At CYVL, the technology we build directly impacts the roads people drive on, the sidewalks t

source_jobs.title: Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Cyvl https://www.cyvl.com/ is a Physical AI company on a mission to bring Infrastructure Intelligence to every city, town, and community on the planet. We help governments plan, build, and maintain the physical infrastructure that people depend on every day, roads, sidewalks, bridges, utilities, and more, better than ever before.

We pioneered an end-to-end approach that combines purpose-built hardware, computer vision, cloud data pipelines, and AI to transform raw LiDAR and imagery into a living, structured understanding of the built world. Our platform turns what used to take months of manual inspection into actionable intelligence that's easy for any agency to use, saving cities time, money, and resources across hundreds of communities nationwide.

At Cyvl, we're building toward a future where every piece of public infrastructure on Earth is captured, understood, and continuously monitored, so the agencies responsible for it and the residents who rely on it can make better decisions, faster. That's the power of Physical AI applied to the real world.

We're a fast-moving, high-growth team based in Boston, MA that believes in solving real-world problems with authenticity, simplicity, and boldness. Every member of our team is empowered to take ownership, deliver results, and leave a lasting impact on the communities we serve.





ABOUT YOU

As a Senior Full-Stack Engineer at Cyvl, you will play a leading role in delivering intuitive, performant, and scalable applications for our customers. You'll be responsible for designing and building across the stack, from elegant, component-driven React interfaces to APIs and services in containerized and serverless environments, backed by relational databases. You'll work in close partnership with product to define and ship features end-to-end, and take real ownership of what you build. The differentiators for us will be a strong technical foundation, a good eye for product, and a genuine enthusiasm for leveraging agents to develop across the stack.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build across the stack, from component-driven React interfaces to APIs, services, and data layers.

 - Design, document, and maintain scalable APIs, and own features end-to-end from conception to deployment.

 - Work with containerized and serverless environments and contribute to cloud infrastructure.

 - Collaborate with design and product teams to deliver smooth, accessible, and responsive interfaces, and partner with QA and DevOps to ensure robust, testable, and observable systems.

 - Contribute to architectural decisions and code reviews across the engineering organization.

 - Mentor junior engineers and elevate the overall engineering bar.
   
   


QUALIFICATIONS


REQUIRED:

 - 5+ years of professional experience in a full-stack engineering role.

 - Proficiency in React and a modern front-end toolchain (e.g., TanStack Router, Vite, pnpm/Turborepo), with a focus on clean, reusable components and highly interactive, data-driven user experiences.

 - Strong command of JavaScript/TypeScript and modern front-end development practices (hooks, context, etc.).

 - Experience building backend services in Python (e.g., FastAPI) and TypeScript (e.g., Cloudflare Workers, Hono), including designing and consuming RESTful and GraphQL APIs.

 - Strong experience with a Postgres-based data layer (e.g., Supabase).

 - Strong Git workflow knowledge and experience with CI/CD pipelines (Github Actions), and experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS, Kubernetes, Cloudflare).

 - Ability to work cross-functionally in a fast-paced, collaborative team environment.





NICE TO HAVE:

 - Experience using real user monitoring and analytics tools (e.g., PostHog, Grafana) to make data-driven development decisions.

 - Expertise in modern Python development (uv, static type checking, Pydantic, asyncio, pytest).

 - Familiarity with event-driven backend architectures using NATS Jetstream.

 - Familiarity with geospatial da

source_jobs.title: Industrial Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

CYVL https://www.cyvl.com/ is a Physical AI company on a mission to bring Infrastructure Intelligence to every city, town, and community on the planet. We help governments plan, build, and maintain the physical infrastructure that people depend on every day (roads, sidewalks, bridges, utilities, and more) better than ever before.

We pioneered an end-to-end approach that combines purpose-built hardware, computer vision, cloud data pipelines, and AI to transform raw LiDAR and imagery into a living, structured understanding of the built world. Our platform turns what used to take months of manual inspection into actionable intelligence that's easy for any agency to use, saving cities time, money, and resources across hundreds of communities nationwide.

At CYVL, we're building toward a future where every piece of public infrastructure on Earth is captured, understood, and continuously monitored, so the agencies responsible for it and the residents who rely on it can make better decisions, faster. That's the power of Physical AI applied to the real world.

We're a fast-moving, high-growth team based in Boston, MA that believes in solving real-world problems with authenticity, simplicity, and boldness. Every member of our team is empowered to take ownership, deliver results, and leave a lasting impact on the communities we serve.

 
 


ABOUT YOU

We map the physical world, and our next-generation sensor is the instrument that does it. We want it to feel less like equipment and more like something you’d be proud to hold. Think Sonos: the weight of a well-made tool, premium outdoor gear that is rugged and beautiful. As our Industrial Designer, you will own the full design vision for the CYVL sensor and prove that high-complexity mapping technology can feel genuinely human: tactile, elegant, and intuitive. This is a contract / consultant engagement (with potential to extend) based in Somerville, MA, hybrid or remote OK, reporting to the Cofounder & Head of Engineering and in close partnership with hardware engineering.

 
 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the full design vision for the CYVL sensor: form, materials, finish, and feel

 - Take shell and enclosure concepts through to refined mockups

 - Make material and tactile decisions that read as premium

 - Iterate tightly with our hardware and engineering team

 - Deliver a handoff package ready to scale with our contract manufacturer

 


QUALIFICATIONS


REQUIRED:

 - Deep industrial design chops: you own the vision, not just the CAD

 - A portfolio that shows restraint, premium materials, and intuitive physical interaction

 - Fluency in CAD, material science, and the design-to-manufacturing loop

 - A “less is more” design philosophy, demonstrated through your work

 


NICE TO HAVE:

 - Experience with products where the object in your hand carries the brand: Sonos, Apple hardware, GoPro, or high-end outdoor equipment

 - A track record of making something complex feel inevitable, and a belief that less is more




OUR VALUES

At CYVL, the technology we build directly impacts the roads people drive on, the sidewalks their kids walk to school on, and the infrastructure that keeps communities running. We move fast, we care deeply: about the work, about our customers, and about each other. This is a team where trust isn't a talking point, it's how we operate.

 

Our values guide how we operate and shape a team that is genuinely RAARE:

Real - authentic, transparent, focused on solving real problems with simple, effective solutions.

All Out - bold, driven, and committed to delivering results that matter.

Accelerating - always learning, growing, and moving with intention.

Reliable - we follow through and deliver at a consistently high bar.

Empowering - we lift each other up and help governments build infrastructure they can be proud of.




READ MORE ABOUT US

Learn about our startup journey https://fortune.com/2025/10/19/meet-gen-z-founder-996-work-ethic-govtech-po

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Cyvl https://www.cyvl.com/ is a Boston-based tech startup revolutionizing the way civil engineering firms and governments map and manage transportation infrastructure. Our enterprise-grade hardware and software solutions leverage 3D mapping sensors to capture LiDAR, imagery, and GPS data, retrofitted to our customers’ vehicles. This data is processed through our AI-powered cloud pipelines to generate actionable condition and geospatial reports, saving our customers time, money, and resources. We are in a phase of rapid growth, disrupting a massive and outdated market with our innovative solutions.

At Cyvl, our mission is to empower governments to build and maintain public infrastructure they’re proud of by accelerating decision-making through our sensors and Infrastructure Intelligence Platform. As our Senior Product Manager, you’ll lead product development across our hardware-enabled software platform and help define what comes next, shipping tools that get used daily by public works teams, engineering consultants, and civic leaders.

We’re a fast-moving, high-growth team that believes in solving real-world problems with authenticity, simplicity, and boldness. Every member of our team is empowered to take ownership, deliver results, and leave a lasting impact on the communities we serve.




ABOUT YOU

We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to lead product development across our hardware-enabled software platform and help define what comes next. This is a highly cross-functional role with big impact: you’ll collaborate with design, engineering, and go-to-market teams to prioritize the most urgent problems and deliver elegant, effective solutions, fast. You’ll partner directly with customers and internal stakeholders to understand key jobs-to-be-done and translate them into smart, focused product decisions. If you’re motivated by shipping real products that help real communities, this role is for you.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Work closely with the leadership team to understand the company’s vision and identify problems to solve that align with that vision

 - Rapidly prototype with Claude Code or similar tools to run discovery with towns and speed up the process from ideas to production code

 - Partner with customers and internal stakeholders to understand key jobs-to-be-done and translate them into smart, focused product decisions

 - Define and track clear success metrics tied to usage, retention, and business growth

 - Work closely with engineering and design to scope work, unblock decisions, and execute with urgency

 - Support GTM efforts by collaborating on launch strategy, pricing experiments, and product demos

 - Help shape the product culture at Cyvl; you’ll be an early and foundational member of the team




QUALIFICATIONS


REQUIRED:

 - 4-7 years of experience in product management or related field in B2B software

 - Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional teams and ship impactful products

 - Strong product sense and customer empathy; you know how to turn vague problems into clear solutions

 - Comfortable digging into data, aligning stakeholders, and making fast, informed trade-offs

 - Technical fluency: you don’t need to be an engineer, but you should understand APIs, data models, and the basics of modern AI/ML workflows




NICE TO HAVE:

 - Experience working on civic tech, infrastructure, GIS, or vertical SaaS

 - Exposure to public sector, urban systems, or field-data workflows




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage

 - 401(k) with immediate vesting

 - Generous PTO, paid parental leave, and flexible work options

 - Annual professional development stipend

 - Team events, referral bonuses, and a collaborative, growth-focused culture




ABOUT CYVL

At Cyvl http://cyvl.com, you’ll join a driven and supportive team that’s all out in building technology with real impact. We care deeply about our work, our customers, and each other. Collaboration an

source_jobs.title: Senior Embedded Systems Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

CYVL is a Physical AI company on a mission to bring Infrastructure Intelligence to every city, town, and community on the planet. We help governments plan, build, and maintain the physical infrastructure that people depend on every day (roads, sidewalks, bridges, utilities, and more) better than ever before.

We pioneered an end-to-end approach that combines purpose-built hardware, computer vision, cloud data pipelines, and AI to transform raw LiDAR and imagery into a living, structured understanding of the built world. Our platform turns what used to take months of manual inspection into actionable intelligence that’s easy for any agency to use, saving cities time, money, and resources across hundreds of communities nationwide.

At CYVL, we’re building toward a future where every piece of public infrastructure on Earth is captured, understood, and continuously monitored so the agencies responsible for it and the residents who rely on it can make better decisions, faster. That’s the power of Physical AI applied to the real world.

We’re a fast-moving, high-growth team based in Boston, MA that believes in solving real-world problems with authenticity, simplicity, and boldness. Every member of our team is empowered to take ownership, deliver results, and leave a lasting impact on the communities we serve.




ABOUT YOU

You’re a builder. You’ve shipped hardware products in scrappy startup environments and you loved doing it. You’re equally comfortable reading a schematic, writing a Linux kernel driver, and debugging a sensor system on the roof of a vehicle in the field. We’re looking for a Senior Embedded Systems Engineer to own the design, build, and productionization of the Cyvl Sensor, our next-generation mobile data collection platform integrating LiDAR, 360-degree cameras, GPS, and IMU on an Nvidia SoM. Your job is to harden it, scale it, and ultimately bring key subsystems in-house as we grow from a handful of units to fleet-scale deployment across tens of thousands of vehicles.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the full hardware platform lifecycle for the Cyvl Sensor: architecture, component selection, PCB/carrier board design, prototyping, testing, and production readiness.

 - Design and integrate multi-sensor systems (LiDAR, cameras, GPS, IMU) with Nvidia Jetson Orin or Thor SoMs, including power delivery, signal integrity, and time synchronization (PTP/PPS).

 - Develop and maintain the embedded Linux BSP: device trees, kernel modules, camera/sensor drivers, and system bring-up on Nvidia JetPack.

 - Drive the transition from off-the-shelf components to custom, in-house designs as the platform scales from prototype to fleet volume.

 - Collaborate with the ML team to ensure the hardware platform supports edge AI inference workloads (model deployment, TensorRT optimization is a plus, not a requirement).

 - Support mechanical and thermal design for ruggedized, fanless, vehicle-mounted enclosures (IP65, wide temperature range, vibration/shock tolerant).




QUALIFICATIONS


REQUIRED

 - 5+ years of experience in embedded systems engineering, hardware design, or a closely related role.

 - Hands-on PCB design experience (schematic capture, layout, bring-up, and debug) with tools such as Altium, KiCad, or Eagle.

 - Deep embedded Linux proficiency: BSP development, device trees, kernel module development, driver authoring (camera, sensor, USB, Ethernet).

 - Experience integrating Nvidia Jetson SoMs (Orin, Xavier, or similar).

 - Experience with GMSL2 or MIPI CSI camera interfaces and high-bandwidth sensor integration.

 - Familiarity with multi-sensor time synchronization (IEEE 1588 PTP, PPS, NTP).

 - Experience with ruggedization: thermal management, vibration/shock mitigation, environmental sealing (IP65+), power architecture for vehicle-mounted systems.

 - Track record of shipping hardware products from prototype or low-volume stage through to reliable, scalable production.

 - Comfortable working in a sta

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Cyvl https://www.cyvl.com/ is a Boston-based tech startup revolutionizing how governments map and manage transportation infrastructure. Our enterprise-grade hardware and software solutions leverage 3D mapping sensors to capture LiDAR, imagery, and GPS data, retrofitted to our customers' vehicles. This data is processed through our AI-powered cloud pipelines to generate actionable geospatial insights that save cities time, money, and resources.



At Cyvl, our mission is to empower governments to build and maintain public infrastructure they’re proud of by accelerating decision-making through our sensors and Infrastructure Intelligence Platform. As a Business Development Representative, you’ll be the first point of contact for municipalities and engineering firms discovering how Cyvl’s technology can transform their infrastructure programs, directly fueling our mission to modernize how communities care for their roads.



We’re a fast-moving, high-growth team that believes in solving real-world problems with authenticity, simplicity, and boldness. Every member of our team is empowered to take ownership, deliver results, and leave a lasting impact on the communities we serve.




ABOUT YOU

You’re a self-starter who thrives on connecting with new people and opening doors. In this role, you’ll drive top-of-funnel growth by identifying and engaging municipalities, DOTs, and civil engineering firms that can benefit from Cyvl’s infrastructure intelligence platform. Your outreach will directly shape our sales pipeline and accelerate Cyvl’s expansion into new markets across the country. This is a full-time, on-site position at our Somerville, MA headquarters starting ASAP.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Identify and research potential leads across municipalities, DOTs, and civil engineering firms to build a robust prospect pipeline

 - Manage outbound communications (email, phone, LinkedIn) and execute multi-channel lead generation campaigns

 - Qualify inbound and outbound leads, schedule discovery calls and demos, and hand off opportunities to the sales team

 - Build and maintain strong relationships with prospective clients while tracking all activity and progress in CRM tools




QUALIFICATIONS


REQUIRED:

 - Proficiency in inside sales and lead generation with a track record of identifying and pursuing new business opportunities

 - Strong sales and business development skills with the ability to build and maintain a growing network of clients

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills to effectively engage with clients and internal teams

 - Strong organizational and time-management skills with the ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment

 - Bachelor’s degree in Business, Sales, Marketing, Psychology, or a related field preferred




NICE TO HAVE:

 - Familiarity with AI tools, CRM platforms (e.g., HubSpot, Salesforce), and sales engagement software

 - Interest in or exposure to government technology, infrastructure, or civic tech industries

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Cyvl https://www.cyvl.com/ is a Boston-based tech startup revolutionizing how civil engineering firms and governments map and manage transportation infrastructure.

Our enterprise-grade hardware and software solutions leverage 3D mapping sensors to capture LiDAR, imagery, and GPS data, retrofitted to our customers' vehicles.

This data is processed through our AI-powered cloud pipelines to generate actionable geospatial insights that save cities and engineering firms time, money, and resources.



At Cyvl, our mission is to empower governments to build and maintain public infrastructure they’re proud of by accelerating decision-making through our sensors and Infrastructure Intelligence Platform. As an Account Executive, you will help bring this mission to life by connecting municipalities with technology that transforms how they manage their transportation networks.



We’re a fast-moving, high-growth team that believes in solving real-world problems with authenticity, simplicity, and boldness. Every member of our team is empowered to take ownership, deliver results, and leave a lasting impact on the communities we serve.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a driven Account Executive who can build pipeline, run disciplined sales cycles, and close mid-market deals with precision and speed. You will own the full revenue motion from prospecting to close, generating predictable, self-sourced pipeline through cold outreach and high-intent follow-up. This role is designed for someone with 1 to 3 years of full-cycle B2B sales experience who wants to sharpen their craft and grow into a Senior AE or leadership role within 18 to 24 months. You will report directly to the VP of Sales and play a key role in expanding Cyvl’s footprint across local governments and engineering firms.





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Source and close mid-market deals with local governments and engineering firms.

 - Prospect relentlessly through calls, emails, and LinkedIn to build a qualified pipeline.

 - Run tight, detail-oriented sales cycles from first meeting through negotiation and close.

 - Maintain accurate CRM data, forecasts, and next-step documentation.

 - Deliver compelling demos and ROI-focused proposals that translate technical value into business outcomes.

 - Partner with Marketing, Product, and Customer Success to ensure smooth handoffs and excellent customer experiences.




QUALIFICATIONS


REQUIRED

 - 1 to 3 years of full-cycle B2B sales experience (SaaS or technology preferred).

 - Track record of meeting or exceeding quotas with average deal sizes of 30K to 150K.

 - Comfortable self-sourcing pipeline and closing your own deals.

 - Exceptional organizational skills and attention to detail.

 - Strong verbal and written communication skills across technical and executive audiences.

 - Experience using HubSpot or Salesforce to manage pipeline and forecast accurately.

 - Self-starter who thrives in fast-paced, high-accountability environments.

 - Hungry to learn, compete, and grow into a Senior AE or leadership role.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience selling into government or civil engineering customers.

 - Familiarity with public sector procurement cycles.

 - Prior experience in early-stage, high-growth tech environments.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage

 - 401(k) with immediate vesting

 - Generous PTO, paid parental leave, and flexible work options

 - Annual professional development stipend

 - Team events, referral bonuses, and a collaborative, growth-focused culture




WHY CYVL

At Cyvl https://www.cyvl.com/, you will join a driven and supportive team that is all out in building technology with real impact. We care deeply about our work, our customers, and each other. Collaboration and trust are central to how we operate.

Our values shape a team that is genuinely RAARE:

source_jobs.title: Head of AI
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Cyvl https://www.cyvl.com/ is a Boston-based tech startup revolutionizing how governments map and manage transportation infrastructure. Our enterprise-grade hardware and software solutions leverage 3D mapping sensors to capture LiDAR, imagery, and GPS data, retrofitted to our customers' vehicles. This data is processed through our AI-powered cloud pipelines to generate actionable geospatial insights that save cities time, money, and resources.

At Cyvl, our mission is to empower governments to build and maintain public infrastructure they're proud of. We are already accelerating decision-making nationwide through our sensors and Infrastructure Intelligence Platform. This role owns the AI/ML strategy that powers the next generation of our Intelligence Platform, translating a bold product vision into the technical roadmap, team, and systems that make it real.

We're a fast-moving, high-growth team that believes in solving real-world problems with authenticity, simplicity, and boldness. Every member of our team is empowered to take ownership, deliver results, and leave a lasting impact on the communities we serve.




ABOUT YOU

You've led AI/ML teams that built production systems on complex, multi-modal data. You've set technical strategy, hired engineers, and shipped products, not just published research. You're comfortable going hands-on with code and models when needed, but your real leverage is in figuring out what to build, in what order, and hiring the right people to build it. You've worked with sensor data, geospatial data, or physical-world AI, and you understand that the hardest problems aren't model architecture problems, they're data strategy problems. You want to build the AI function that teaches machines to understand physical infrastructure at a depth and scale that has never existed.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Interpret Cyvl's Infrastructure Intelligence vision and translate it into a concrete AI/ML technical strategy covering perception (imagery + LiDAR), knowledge representation, and data architecture.

 - Be hands-on in the early months: set up the initial ML pipelines, make key architectural decisions, and validate technical approaches before you have a team.

 - Hire and lead the AI/ML team, starting with a senior ML engineer and a senior knowledge graph architect as the first two roles.

 - Own the full intelligence extraction pipeline: from raw sensor data to detections, geometric measurements, cross-modal representations, and a queryable knowledge graph.

 - Work directly with the CEO and engineering leadership to align AI/ML investments with product priorities and customer needs.




QUALIFICATIONS


REQUIRED

 - 8+ years in ML/AI, with at least 3 years leading a team (hiring, managing, setting technical direction).

 - Track record of shipping production ML systems on sensor data, geospatial data, or multi-modal data at scale.

 - Experience setting AI/ML strategy for a product or business unit, not just executing someone else's roadmap.

 - Hands-on technical depth: you can train models, design data pipelines, and evaluate architectural tradeoffs yourself.

 - Experience with data-efficient learning approaches (active learning, semi-supervised methods, foundation model adaptation) in production.

 - Led teams of 3+ ML/AI engineers and made successful senior technical hires.

 - Strong communicator who can translate between technical AI/ML concepts and business/product strategy.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with LiDAR, point cloud data, or 3D perception.

 - Background in autonomous vehicles, HD mapping, satellite imagery, or infrastructure/civil engineering domains.

 - Experience building knowledge graphs or ontology-driven data architectures.

 - Familiarity with geospatial databases (PostGIS, spatial indexing).

 - Experience designing data architectures for agentic or LLM-driven access patterns.

 - Background at Palantir, an AV company, or a geospatial intelligence organization.

 -

source_jobs.title: Senior Account Executive - NYC
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Cyvl http://cyvl.com is a Boston-based tech startup revolutionizing how civil engineering firms and governments map and manage transportation infrastructure. Our enterprise-grade hardware and software solutions leverage 3D mapping sensors to capture LiDAR, imagery, and GPS data, retrofitted to our customers' vehicles. This data is processed through our AI-powered cloud pipelines to generate actionable geospatial insights that save cities and engineering firms time, money, and resources.

At Cyvl, our mission is to empower governments to build and maintain public infrastructure they are proud of by accelerating decision-making through our sensors and Infrastructure Intelligence Platform. You will be part of a team of A+ players solving hard, meaningful problems and working directly with leaders at top cities and engineering firms.

We are a fast-moving, high-growth team that believes in solving real-world problems with authenticity, simplicity, and boldness. Every member of our team is empowered to take ownership, deliver results, and leave a lasting impact on the communities we serve.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a hungry, smart, and relentless full-cycle seller who can build and close high-value enterprise and public sector deals. You will own every stage of the sales motion from prospecting to close across city, county, and engineering-firm accounts, engaging directly with executives and multi-stakeholder buying teams. This role offers a front-row seat to how a startup scales from approximately 5M ARR into a 50M+ category leader.

You will partner closely with Product, Marketing, and Customer Success to turn wins into long-term strategic relationships and help elevate the performance of the overall sales function.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build and manage a seven-figure pipeline across city, county, and enterprise accounts.

 - Run disciplined discovery using MEDDICC to qualify, quantify, and close.

 - Navigate multi-stakeholder procurement and RFPs with precision.

 - Consistently close $2M+ in new ARR with ACVs of $150K to 1M+.

 - Forecast accurately and keep CRM spotless.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally and coach junior reps to raise the bar.




QUALIFICATIONS


REQUIRED

 - 3 to 5 years of full-cycle B2B sales experience, ideally in enterprise SaaS or public sector.

 - Proven track record of closing complex six-figure or larger deals.

 - Strong communication skills with both technical and executive-level audiences.

 - Competitive, curious, and highly coachable. Thrives in office environments, on whiteboards, and on the road.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience selling to government agencies or civil engineering firms.

 - Experience in early stage, high-growth startup environments.

 - Familiarity with procurement cycles, RFP processes, and MEDDICC.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage

 - 401(k) with immediate vesting

 - Generous PTO, paid parental leave, and flexible work options

 - Annual professional development stipend

 - Team events, referral bonuses, and a collaborative, growth-focused culture




WHY CYVL

At Cyvl http://cyvl.com, you’ll join a driven and supportive team that’s all out in building technology with real impact. We care deeply about our work, our customers, and each other. Collaboration and trust are at the core of how we operate.

Our values guide how we operate and shape a team that is genuinely RAARE:

 - Real - authentic, transparent, focused on solving real problems with simple, effective solutions.

 - All Out - bold, driven, and committed to delivering results that matter.

 - Accelerating - always learning, growing, and moving with intention.

 - Reliable - we follow through and deliver at a consistently high bar.

 - Empowering - we lift each other up and help governments build infrastructure they can be proud of.

Together, we stay curious, learn quickly, and keep raising the bar. If you want to grow, make an impact that truly matt

source_jobs.title: Head of Hardware
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

CYVL https://www.cyvl.com/ is a Physical AI company on a mission to bring Infrastructure Intelligence to every city, town, and community on the planet. We help governments plan, build, and maintain the physical infrastructure that people depend on every day, roads, sidewalks, bridges, utilities, and more, better than ever before.

We pioneered an end-to-end approach that combines purpose-built hardware, computer vision, cloud data pipelines, and AI to transform raw LiDAR and imagery into a living, structured understanding of the built world. Our platform turns what used to take months of manual inspection into actionable intelligence that's easy for any agency to use, saving cities time, money, and resources across hundreds of communities nationwide.

At CYVL, we're building toward a future where every piece of public infrastructure on Earth is captured, understood, and continuously monitored, so the agencies responsible for it and the residents who rely on it can make better decisions, faster. That's the power of Physical AI applied to the real world.

We're a fast-moving, high-growth team based in Boston, MA that believes in solving real-world problems with authenticity, simplicity, and boldness. Every member of our team is empowered to take ownership, deliver results, and leave a lasting impact on the communities we serve.




ABOUT YOU

Cyvl is scaling its capture hardware from a hand-built sensor to a contract-manufactured platform, and the Head of Hardware owns that transition end to end. You will define and drive the hardware strategy for V5 and the generations that follow, take our next-generation sensor from prototype through its first contract-manufactured production run, and build the lean team that delivers it. You will own vendor selection, the bill of materials, supply chain, and the contract manufacturing relationship, while holding the line on cost, quality, security, and reliability for national deployment across government fleets. This is the role that turns Cyvl's hardware bet into the durable foundation for our national infrastructure data layer.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own Cyvl's hardware strategy and roadmap: platform architecture, sensor integration, modular design, the build-vs-buy posture, and the generational roadmap beyond V5.

 - Lead vendor selection and BOM component procurement, and own all vendor relationships and supply chain timelines through to production.

 - Stand up and manage the contract manufacturing relationship, engaging the CM partner early and locking the first production run.

 - Build and lead a best-in-class lean hardware team, delivering rock-solid firmware and an unparalleled hardware UX.

 - Drive the scaled BOM cost down to at-scale targets while protecting quality, security, and reliability for national government deployment.




QUALIFICATIONS


REQUIRED:

 - 10+ years of hardware engineering experience, including time leading hardware teams or programs.

 - Has personally shipped at least one hardware product at scale, from design through high-volume production and into the field.

 - Proven track record taking hardware or sensor products from prototype to scaled, contract-manufactured production.

 - Deep experience owning vendor selection, BOM management, supply chain, and contract manufacturing relationships.

 - Experience leading and growing cross-functional hardware teams across embedded, firmware, electrical, and mechanical disciplines.

 - Strong systems judgment on build-vs-buy and COTS strategy, with a bias toward riding the cost curve rather than moving to custom builds before volume justifies it.

 - Hands-on familiarity with sensor stacks (LiDAR, cameras, GNSS/IMU) and edge compute platforms such as NVIDIA Jetson/Orin.

 - Bachelor's or higher in electrical, mechanical, computer, or systems engineering, or equivalent hands-on experience.


NICE TO HAVE:

 - Experience deploying hardware into government, defense-adjacent, or otherwise regulated a

notes: Cyvl is a Physical AI company with a strong focus on engineering and product development.
